description: Fix four friction points in the plugin developer scaffold and CLI: (1) the non-interactive error message hardcodes the wrong invocation command for npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin users, (2) jiti is listed as an unused devDependency in every scaffolded plugin, (3) the dev watch script uses bash-only & syntax that silently breaks on Windows, and (4) opentabs plugin configure crashes with an unhandled top-level await error when run in non-TTY/piped mode.

userStories.title: Fix scaffold non-interactive error message to reference correct invocation command
userStories.description: The promptForMissingArgs function in scaffold.ts hardcodes 'Usage: opentabs plugin create <name> --domain <domain>' in the non-interactive error message. When the user invokes the scaffold via 'npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin --domain example.com' (omitting name), they see usage instructions for the wrong command. Fix by making the usage/example line configurable so create-plugin passes its own binary name.
acceptanceCriteria: promptForMissingArgs accepts an optional invocationName parameter (defaults to 'opentabs plugin create') used in the error message
create-plugin/src/index.ts passes 'npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin' as invocationName to promptForMissingArgs
cli/src/commands/plugin.ts passes no invocationName (uses default 'opentabs plugin create')
Error message for missing name when invoked via create-plugin shows 'npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin' not 'opentabs plugin create'
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts (add optional invocationName param to promptForMissingArgs signature and error message)
- platform/create-plugin/src/index.ts (pass invocationName)
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (no change needed since default matches)

Context:
The error is thrown in promptForMissingArgs when process.stdin.isTTY is falsy and name or domain is missing. Current code at platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 436-441:

```typescript
if (!process.stdin.isTTY) {
  const missing = [!name && 'name', !domain && '--domain'].filter(Boolean);
  throw new ScaffoldError(
    `Missing required arguments: ${missing.join(', ')}.\n\nUsage: opentabs plugin create <name> --domain <domain>\nExample: opentabs plugin create my-app --domain .example.com --display "My App"`,
  );
}
```

The `promptForMissingArgs` function signature is:
```typescript
const promptForMissingArgs = async (partial: PartialScaffoldArgs): Promise<ScaffoldArgs>
```

create-plugin/src/index.ts calls it as:
```typescript
const args = await promptForMissingArgs({ name, domain: options.domain, ... });
```

Changes:
1. Add `invocationName?: string` to PartialScaffoldArgs interface (or add a separate param)
2. Update the error message to use `invocationName ?? 'opentabs plugin create'`
3. In create-plugin/src/index.ts, pass `invocationName: 'npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin'`

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the PartialScaffoldArgs type exported from scaffold.ts — this type is re-exported. Add invocationName as a separate optional param to the function instead of adding it to PartialScaffoldArgs if that causes issues.
- The export of promptForMissingArgs stays the same.

userStories.title: Remove unused jiti devDependency from scaffolded plugin package.json
userStories.description: The generatePackageJson function in scaffold.ts includes 'jiti: "^2.6.1"' in devDependencies of every scaffolded plugin. jiti is never referenced in any plugin script, build tool, or source file — it was likely left over from an earlier build approach. Remove it from the scaffold template.
acceptanceCriteria: jiti no longer appears in the devDependencies section of generatePackageJson in scaffold.ts
Scaffolded plugin package.json does not include jiti as a dependency
No other scaffold file references jiti
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts (remove jiti from devDependencies object)

Context:
The devDependencies object in generatePackageJson at platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 185-191:

```typescript
devDependencies: {
  '@biomejs/biome': '2.4.5',
  '@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools': openTabsVersion,
  jiti: '^2.6.1',
  typescript: '^5.9.3',
  zod: zodVersion,
},
```

Changes:
Delete the `jiti: '^2.6.1',` line. No other changes needed.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove any other devDependency — only jiti. Every other entry is used.

userStories.title: Fix dev watch script Windows incompatibility by replacing & with concurrently
userStories.description: The scaffolded plugin's dev npm script uses 'tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch'. The & operator is bash-specific — on Windows, npm scripts run in cmd.exe where & is a sequential operator, not background. As a result, on Windows tsc --watch blocks and opentabs-plugin build --watch never starts. Fix by replacing & with concurrently, adding it as a devDependency in the scaffold.
acceptanceCriteria: generatePackageJson in scaffold.ts includes 'concurrently: "^9.0.0"' in devDependencies
The dev script in generatePackageJson uses 'concurrently "tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput" "opentabs-plugin build --watch"'
The dev script does not use the & operator
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts (change dev script and add concurrently devDependency)

Context:
Current dev script in generatePackageJson at platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts line 171:
```typescript
dev: 'tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch',
```

Current devDependencies (after US-002 removes jiti) at lines 185-191:
```typescript
devDependencies: {
  '@biomejs/biome': '2.4.5',
  '@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools': openTabsVersion,
  typescript: '^5.9.3',
  zod: zodVersion,
},
```

Changes:
1. Change dev script to: 'concurrently "tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput" "opentabs-plugin build --watch"'
2. Add 'concurrently: "^9.0.0"' to devDependencies

Constraints:
- Use concurrently version ^9.0.0 (current stable)
- Do NOT add --kill-others or other concurrently flags — keep the script minimal
- Do NOT change the build, type-check, lint, format:check, or check scripts

userStories.title: Fix plugin configure crash on non-TTY stdin
userStories.description: handlePluginConfigure in plugin.ts does not check process.stdin.isTTY before entering interactive readline prompts. When stdin is piped (non-interactive, e.g. CI, scripted usage), rl.question() hits EOF, and the required-field loop keeps spinning until Node.js crashes with 'Detected unsettled top-level await'. Add a non-TTY guard at the start of handlePluginConfigure that exits with a helpful error directing the user to 'opentabs config set setting.<plugin>.<key> <value>'.
acceptanceCriteria: When stdin is non-TTY, the command exits with code 1 and prints an error message
The error message includes the alternative command: 'opentabs config set setting.<plugin>.<key> <value>'
When stdin is a TTY, the configure flow works as before
